nodes.label: AI as rapid prototyping tool for vague briefs
nodes.label_negative: Manual coding from scratch without AI assistance
nodes.grounding_span: quickly mock up interfaces in React, before fine tuning and creating the more technical detail myself

nodes.label: AI adherence to explicit requirements
nodes.grounding_span: AI is usually very good at hitting the explicit goals, if something is written in the brief it'll almost certainly be included. However implicit requirements occasionally get missed

nodes.label: AI reliability in code generation
nodes.label_negative: AI generating outdated or error-prone code
nodes.grounding_span: commands to build the React project (these often are outdated and do not work well with Tailwind CSS) or occasional minor exception errors in the code

nodes.label: AI usefulness for tasks of moderate complexity
nodes.label_negative: AI inefficiency for very simple or very complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: both the extremely technical and extremely basic functions I generally choose to handle myself

nodes.label: AI as task delegator for complete subtasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as collaborative partner on large tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I generally delegate it complete tasks, such as small functions or working on individual components

nodes.label: AI integration encouraged by small company culture
nodes.label_negative: AI adoption hindered by large company inertia
nodes.grounding_span: My company is relatively small (less than 10 people), so we've been encouraged to embrace it quite a lot

nodes.label: AI satisfaction from complete task completion
nodes.label_negative: AI frustration from prolonged error correction
nodes.grounding_span: AI is mainly satisfying when it's able to complete a task in its entirety without correction

nodes.label: AI leading to skill atrophy through over-reliance
nodes.label_negative: AI preserving or enhancing skills through practice
nodes.grounding_span: I do see a certain laziness appear in my work sometimes where I get AI to do something relatively trivial simply because I can't be bothered

nodes.label: AI as job threat through full automation
nodes.label_negative: AI as job complement creating new opportunities
nodes.grounding_span: why would I be needed if my manager could just feed his requirements into a model that could generate the entire project from scratch

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: that feeling of just having the job done in a few moments when it might have taken you 30+ minutes to put together yourself is great

nodes.label: Quality and correctness of output
nodes.grounding_span: compare it to the brief to ensure compliance

nodes.label: Professional competence and skill retention
nodes.grounding_span: this will lead to the loss of skills through lack of practice

nodes.label: Job security and career sustainability
nodes.grounding_span: I hope that my job is not completely eradicated

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for rapid prototyping
nodes.grounding_span: quickly mock up interfaces in React, before fine tuning and creating the more technical detail myself

nodes.label: Frustration with AI missing implicit requirements
nodes.grounding_span: implicit requirements occasionally get missed

nodes.label: Frustration with AI code errors and outdated commands
nodes.grounding_span: commands to build the React project (these often are outdated and do not work well with Tailwind CSS) or occasional minor exception errors

nodes.label: Preference for handling simple and complex tasks manually
nodes.grounding_span: both the extremely technical and extremely basic functions I generally choose to handle myself

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI completing tasks without correction
nodes.grounding_span: AI is mainly satisfying when it's able to complete a task in its entirety without correction

nodes.label: Frustration with prolonged AI error correction
nodes.grounding_span: it takes forever (in terms of corrections) to get a code block to run without error

nodes.label: Concern about skill atrophy due to AI overuse
nodes.grounding_span: I do see a certain laziness appear in my work sometimes where I get AI to do something relatively trivial simply because I can't be bothered

nodes.label: Optimistic historical perspective on AI revolution
nodes.grounding_span: I see the AI revolution in much the same way as when computers became widespread - many jobs were eradicated but many new ones were also created

nodes.label: Task decomposition into delegable subtasks
nodes.grounding_span: I generally delegate it complete tasks, such as small functions or working on individual components

nodes.label: Comparative efficiency assessment before engaging AI
nodes.grounding_span: it would take too long to explain the function to a model and the risk of it generating an error is too large
